content.claim: Non-census-based special education funding systems correlate with increases in SEN provision, whereas census-based systems remove fiscal incentives to identify students as having SEN; Finland's 2010 funding reform shifted toward census-based allocation and reduced full-time special-education placement.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Causal direction not fully isolated; municipalities may adopt census-based funding in response to already-rising identification
Study does not quantify how much of Finland's post-reform identification rise was attributable to funding-system change versus other factors
Generalizability to Hungary depends on structural similarity of funding incentives, which requires separate analysis
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
